title: Governance Authority Hierarchy
purpose: Implement a canonical governance authority hierarchy system that eliminates duplicated governance semantics, eliminates contradictory authority, establishes canonical ownership rules, defines governance precedence, and reduces governance entropy.
architectural_constraints: Layer 0 schemas are machine authority and cannot be overridden
Lower layers override higher layers on specificity
Projections (Layer 4) are read-only and never mutative
Human guides (Layer 5) are lossy and must not contradict layers 0-3
Every document must declare explicit authority metadata

deliverables.description: Layered authority model (Layer 0-5) with precedence rules, cross-silo supremacy, conflict resolution procedure
JSON Schema for authority frontmatter validation
Reduced to principles-only; delegates operational specifics to protocols
Validator that checks all governance docs have authority frontmatter

acceptance_criteria: All 20+ governance documents have YAML authority frontmatter
CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY.md defines 6 layers with precedence rules
No auto-authorization contradiction remains in kernel
npm run pm:validate passes with 0 errors
Authority validator script exits 0 for all docs

traceability.description: Implemented canonical authority hierarchy: layered model, frontmatter on 23 docs, kernel reduction, deprecated policy, authority validator

failure_modes.mitigation: Run validate-authority.js in CI pre-commit hook

failure_modes.scenario: New governance doc added without frontmatter
failure_modes.mitigation: CI gate blocks merge on missing frontmatter

metadata.operational_rationale: Without explicit authority hierarchy, governance documents drift into contradiction. This was observed in the auto-authorization conflict between kernel and EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION_PROTOCOL.md.
